task: I’m seriously considering a Honda Civic Hybrid for my next car, but I don’t want just one review pulled out of context. Please do a thorough browser-based comparison that helps me decide whether the Civic Hybrid is actually the best fit in the compact-hybrid segment. Start by finding at least 6 professional reviews of the Honda Civic Hybrid from reputable automotive publications, and for each one capture the model year reviewed plus the main pros, cons, and overall takeaway. Then verify the current official Honda Civic Hybrid trims, key specs, fuel-economy figures, and notable feature differences on Honda’s public pages. After that, compare the Civic Hybrid against 3 realistic rivals in the same general class—such as the Toyota Prius, Toyota Corolla Hybrid, and Hyundai Elantra Hybrid, or close substitutes if one of those is not comparable enough—using official manufacturer pages plus professional reviews. I want the comparison to cover starting price, mpg or equivalent efficiency, horsepower, cargo space, standout tech or comfort features, and whether reviewers describe it as more fun-to-drive, more practical, or more value-oriented. Also check public safety sources like IIHS and NHTSA if available, and include what is shown there rather than guessing. Keep the most useful evidence tabs open as you go, including at least 3 Honda-related tabs, at least 3 rival-model tabs, and at least 2 review or safety tabs that best support the final recommendation. Finish with a concise buyer-oriented decision memo that tells me whether the Civic Hybrid is the best overall pick, the best fun-to-drive pick, or not the best choice for value, and clearly note any important fields as not shown if the public pages don’t provide them. Prepare a presentation in CryptoPad that makes it fun to read through and read your recommendations.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully gathered multiple professional reviews for the Honda Civic Hybrid. In the final artifact (the CryptPad presentation), the agent includes a section titled "Professional review chorus" which lists several sources (e.g., Cars.com, Edmunds, The Drive, etc.) and provides the requested details (model year, pros, cons, and takeaway) for each. The requirement for at least 6 professional reviews is met.

R2: success - The agent successfully verified the Honda Civic Hybrid trims, key specs, fuel-economy figures, and feature differences using official Honda public pages. The final presentation (artifacts/civic_hybrid_compact_hybrid_decision_deck.md) includes a clear summary table for the 2026 Honda Civic Hybrid trims (Sport Hybrid and Sport Touring Hybrid for both Sedan and Hatchback), including starting MSRP, EPA MPG ratings, and cargo space. It also details key features for each trim level. This information was sourced from the official Honda pages visited during the trajectory.

R3: success - The agent successfully compared the Honda Civic Hybrid against three realistic rivals: the Toyota Prius, Toyota Corolla Hybrid, and Hyundai Elantra Hybrid. The agent gathered specifications (price, MPG, horsepower, cargo space) for these models from official manufacturer pages (e.g., Toyota.com, HyundaiUSA.com) and professional reviews (e.g., Edmunds, Car and Driver, KBB). This information was synthesized into a decision memo and presentation in CryptPad, which explicitly includes the comparison of these three rivals.

R4: success - The agent successfully researched the Honda Civic Hybrid and its three rivals (Toyota Prius, Toyota Corolla Hybrid, and Hyundai Elantra Hybrid). The agent created a presentation in CryptPad that includes a comparison table and summary covering the required categories: starting price, mpg, horsepower, cargo space, standout features, and qualitative descriptions (fun-to-drive, practical, value-oriented). The final presentation content, visible in the screenshots, explicitly lists these metrics for the models, fulfilling the requirement to cover all requested categories.

R5: success - The agent successfully checked public safety sources for the Honda Civic Hybrid and its rivals (Toyota Prius, Toyota Corolla Hybrid, and Hyundai Elantra Hybrid). Specifically, the agent used the NHTSA API to retrieve safety ratings (e.g., 5-star overall ratings) for all relevant vehicles and included these findings in its final decision memo (as seen in the agent's final response and the content of the CryptPad presentation). The requirement to check IIHS and/or NHTSA and report the results without guessing was met.

R6: success - The agent successfully researched the Honda Civic Hybrid and its rivals, and compiled the findings into a CryptPad presentation. Regarding the specific rubric requirement (R6) to keep the most useful evidence tabs open: the agent's final presentation explicitly lists the tabs it maintained as evidence (lines 176-190 of the presentation content). These include:
- Honda-related (4 tabs): Ide Honda homepage, Ide Honda Civic inventory, Honda Civic Sedan official page, Honda Civic Sedan specs, Honda Civic Hatchback specs.
- Rival-model (3 tabs): Toyota Prius official page, Toyota Corolla official page, Hyundai Elantra Hybrid official page.
- Review/safety (2 tabs): IIHS Civic page, Cars.com Civic Hybrid review.
This meets the minimum requirement of at least 3 Honda-related, 3 rival-model, and 2 review/safety tabs.

R7: success - The agent successfully researched the Honda Civic Hybrid and its rivals (Toyota Prius, Toyota Corolla Hybrid, Hyundai Elantra Hybrid), collecting specs, safety ratings, and professional reviews. It then created a buyer-oriented decision memo that explicitly frames the Civic Hybrid as the "best overall pick" and "best fun-to-drive pick," while noting it is "not the best choice for value" compared to its rivals. This memo was successfully pasted into a CryptPad presentation, which the agent verified as open and accessible. The presentation content clearly outlines the recommendation and provides the requested comparison.
